tweets.text: We are looking for excellent people to help build our vertically integrated AI stack. Numerics, quantization, HW simulators, compiler, runtime, kernel performance, RTL, verification, emulation, DFT, physical design, post Si bringup. Join us at Tesla!
Tesla self-driving works very well on narrow roads with oncoming large tractors!
Tesla FSD is now rolling out to actual customers in the Netherlands!

A fitting analogy for the pace at which Tesla has been making progress over the years is that of a Formula One car, whose peak acceleration increases in proportion to its speed as a result of enhanced traction generated by aerodynamic downforce. Our goal is to get Optimus to
Can’t have a more exciting vision for the future!! Come join us to make this the reality. To the stars, let’s go
Extreme AI software - AI hardware - Semiconductor fabrication co-design!

Tesla FSD is nothing short of magic. Just rented a Cybertruck, picked it up at the airport and it drove us directly to the Airbnb. None of the stress of a new city, new roads, new car. Had it for 5 days and never drove myself. It drove perfectly. So easy and liberating
We are actively hiring talented people to work on this extremely high impact project. Join @Tesla_AI or @xai to help accelerate this.
It really is! And the upcoming tech will make it even more so.

Bought a Tesla yesterday and I’m already blown away by how incredible the self driving function is. I’m even more convinced now that within a generation driving a car is going to be like riding a horse, something a small minority of people do for fun.

That's right! I recently bought a Model Y Juniper specifically for FSD. I am 82 and my driving reactions are not as quick as years ago. My wife had taken to white-knuckling the panic bar on her door when I drove. With FSD and me still "behind the wheel" she's totally calm. We are
Model Y on FSD (Supervised) v14.2.2.5 identifies a possible collision ahead of the car in front of it and reacts accordingly.

I just got back from a 4 hour demo drive in a new Tesla Model Y Premium and I’m still kind of buzzing. I've watched the videos. I’ve read the stories. I thought I understood what FSD was. I did not, not even close! Because when you’re actually in it and you let it do its thing,

I only did a short Uber "shift" today and went ahead and wrapped up early for Sunday night and I'm back home. You may ask what's important about that to you JC? Well, let me tell you. As of today, it has now been exactly one year since my last critical intervention with Tesla
Second Robotaxi ride without safety monitor in the heart of Austin

Robotaxi rides without any safety monitors are now publicly available in Austin. Starting with a few unsupervised vehicles mixed in with the broader robotaxi fleet with safety monitors, and the ratio will increase over time.
Tesla will stop selling FSD after Feb 14. FSD will only be available as a monthly subscription thereafter.
Happy New Year!! 2026 will be a historical year! Effects of AI will be felt in a visceral manner by common people.
World’s first fully autonomous coast-to-coast drive, done with Tesla self-driving v14. Congrats and thank you @DavidMoss !
If you are at NeurIPS, try driving around in our synthetic world.

Not to mention, these chips are heavily co-designed with the @Tesla_AI software teams to achieve incredible performance, while also beating by a large margin on perf per Watt and perf per dollar. For instance, AI4 can process and understand a million pixels of streaming video

This note regarding the importance of Elon leading Tesla is more relevant now than ever. Tesla is at a critical juncture, as it is metamorphosing into the world leader in robotics. Creating large-scale, useful robots requires expertise across engineering design, manufacturing,

I just did my first serious V14 drive on version 14.1.4. TLDR: I’m super happy with it. V12 was the upgrade that made highway roadtrips completely stress free and V14 feels like the upgrade that brings that to everywhere else. Legitimate safety interventions are going to be

Wrote about some of the recent work from the excellent @Tesla_AI team. The same material was presented at the International Conference of Computer Vision this week. The main purpose of sharing this content is to show the cutting-edge work that the team has been developing and
FSD v14.1.3: Not sure what’s more wild - this truck backing up at a red light or FSD handling it perfectly by reversing to avoid a collision and then proceeding once clear
For those who are still skeptical of using cameras + neural nets to drive the car, this video is the antithesis.
For when you’re late to your flight, or to pick your kid from school, and so on.
FSD v14.1.2, going to early access today, will debut a much awaited feature
Tesla self-driving can reverse to get out of potentially dangerous positions.

Many more Tesla self-driving V14 improvements still to come, but wide rollout of V14.1 has begun. 14.2 rolls out in a few weeks and then 14.3 a few weeks later, depending on safety testing. There is so much change that we are carefully confirming each one.

FSD 14.1, the first in the v14 series, has officially started rolling out to customers! Many follow up releases, with significant improvements, are in the works. These should ship through the rest of this year. Enjoy, and looking forward to your feedback!

Just the beginning! Once the AI models for self-driving and Optimus unify it’s going to be It’s a great time to join the @Tesla_AI team to work on the coolest products on the planet!! http:// tesla.com/AI
In this modern world, the cost of food isn’t money, but exercise.
search and feed unified into a single thing would be
would be cool to just chat with the X algorithm, like “don’t show me any of swift kelce engagement things” and it just cleans up the feed
I know I’m supposed to be adult and stuff, but the Dog Man books are super hilarious
Credit to @stevenmarkryan for the tip. He needs to come down and take a ride

Austin's Robotaxi service gets a review from a non-Tesla "influencer" What's better than a ~$15 Uber ride? A $4.20 Robotaxi ride in a driverless Model Y. The rider even mentioned that it's smoother than typical Ubers he has ridden in the past.
I just accidentally fell asleep in Robotaxi I’m usually a terrible passenger, but it lulled me to sleep. people have no idea what’s coming

Not sure if it’s clear in the video: the white MY ahead at the factory is also driverless! It’s one of the many cars that drive autonomously from the end of the factory line to a parking lot to wait for delivery to customers. @Jagarzaf ’s car just had a slightly different
World's first autonomous delivery of a car! This Tesla drove itself from Gigafactory Texas to its new owner's home ~30min away — crossing parking lots, highways & the city to reach its new owner
Thanks Jose for participating in our first autonomous delivery mission! Please take care good care of our little one.
I was the one that received it! Did not drive back!
I’m very, very impressed with Tesla Robotaxi. Videos online don’t do justice. Sometimes you’ve got to experience it in person to fully appreciate it, and this is one of those cases.
10 years in the making! Incredible work from the entire @Tesla team to get to this milestone.
Zero drama. Smooth & comfortable. Tesla team should be so proud.

Thank you @SecDuffy and team for visiting us! It was a very productive visit and looking forward to working closely with @USDOT and @NHTSAgov as we usher in the next wave of safe and affordable transportation for all of the United States.

Will need this big time in the future. With autonomous vehicles we'll have affordable premium transport for everyone. This will likely increase traffic due to the increased usage, even though each vehicle is much more efficiently utilized.

Full Self Driving is literally the greatest technology I’ve ever used Period I get 2 hours back of brain power every day to focus on my business while my car drives me You have legit no idea until you use it what a game changer it is No way I can go back to regular driving
FSD’s end-to-end model continues to scale well to other regions. First China, now Europe.

If you want to succeed in life you have to lock in your morning routine. Here is mine: 1. Wake up (arguably most important) 2. Stumble into my Tesla 3. Have FSD drive me to the office 4. Barely in time for the first meeting
1000s of Teslas drive themselves each week to the loading docks!
Shows the scalability of Tesla’s generalized solution to autonomy. No HD maps, no sensor over load, just looks outside and drives, like humans.
Same. The Grok app imo has the most delightful user experience in recent times

Just witnessed a scary accident on the Sea-to-Sky Highway to Whistler. FSD computer reacted in milliseconds, steering me away safely while preventing hydroplaning. The car’s cameras captured it all. Drive safe, everyone! #Tesla #FSD @Tesla_AI @wholemars
Just a matter of time before it's rolling on public roads

MUST WATCH: FSD V13.2.2! Interesting new behavior -Detects heavy braking up ahead. -Performs a safety dive into the shoulder lane to stop safely and avoid a potential accident. -Once the path is clear, it seamlessly merges back into regular traffic and continues driving.

Extremely bad visibility passing through the storm here. Lots of water on the road car is literally hydroplaning. Bad road markings in this area, a mix of new and old. I was a little worried. No problem for FSD v13!! Just Perfection! @elonmusk @aelluswamy @wholemars

FSD 12.6 Initial Impressions Great news! You’re gonna love this version coming from 12.5.4.2. I received it on my Plaid Model S last night. Spent 20 minutes driving a mixture of city streets and interstate freeway just now. I have the benefit of an A->B comparison courtesy

Just finished my initial drive with FSD (Supervised) V12.6 on my AI3 2022 Model X. I am very impressed with it! It is a very confident build - a vast improvement on 12.5.4.2. I did not experience any unusual slowdowns or wheel jerkiness. It is very confident and quick to start
Great showcase of FSD 13’s abilities, driving through dense Manhattan traffic.
